name: Chemprop (Message Passing Neural Networks for Molecular Property Prediction)

performance.notes: PyTorch GPU-accelerated; batch molecular property prediction

agent_readiness.notes: Message passing neural network (MPNN/D-MPNN) for molecular property prediction. from chemprop import data, models, nn, featurizers. smiles_data = [chemprop.data.MoleculeDatapoint.from_smi(smi, y=target) for ...]. featurizer = chemprop.featurizers.SimpleMoleculeMolGraphFeaturizer(). train_data = chemprop.data.MoleculeDataset(smiles_data). model = chemprop.models.MPNN(chemprop.nn.BondMessagePassing(), chemprop.nn.MeanAggregation(), chemprop.nn.RegressionFFN()). Chemprop CLI: chemprop train --data_path data.csv --smiles_column smiles --target_columns activity. Atom features. Calibration.

gotchas: Chemprop 2.0 changed API significantly from 1.x — tutorials may be outdated
Hyperparameter tuning (depth, hidden_size, ffn_layers) required for best results
CLI is simpler than Python API for standard tasks
best_when: Molecular property prediction with MPNN (best-in-class on many benchmarks)
When D-MPNN architecture is needed for direct-message passing
avoid_when: Simple fingerprint-based models (use sklearn + RDKit Morgan FP)
summary: Chemprop implements D-MPNN message passing neural networks for molecular property prediction. CLI: chemprop train --data_path data.csv. Python: MoleculeDataset + MPNN(BondMessagePassing, MeanAggregation, RegressionFFN). Atom features. Calibration. MIT by MIT Barzilay Group. Best-in-class on many molecular property benchmarks (ESOL, Lipophilicity, BACE). Chemprop 2.0 changed API from 1.x significantly.
